current.scope: Extra-EU27 trade (declarant=EU, partner ≠ EU27), Comext CN 15/22/24/25–39 subset

34.label: Soap, organic surface-active agents, washing preparations, lubricating preparations, artificial waxes, prepared waxes, polishing or scouring preparations, candles and similar articles, modelling pastes, ‘dental waxes’ and dental preparations with a basis of plaster

data.source: Cefic analysis based on Comext data (Eurostat, 2026)

editorial_context.scope_note: Extra-EU27 trade (declarant=EU, partner ≠ EU27), Comext CN 15/22/24/25–39 subset

comext_ipi_divergence.interpretation: Trade shock magnitudes of this order normally co-move with production or price shocks. The absence of such co-movement suggests a base effect or a one-off composition change rather than a real underlying rupture.

value_volume_divergence.interpretation: Value decline exceeds volume decline by more than the 2.0× threshold, indicating a unit-value / product-mix shift rather than a uniform demand contraction.

partner_concentration.interpretation: 95.2% of the absolute variation is explained by 2 partners. A movement this concentrated is not a structural trend — it is an idiosyncratic shift in a small number of bilateral flows.

abnormal_prior_year_base.interpretation: The comparison base (2025-02) is 4.8 standard deviations above the mean of the preceding 12 months. Any YoY change against this base will mechanically exaggerate the apparent movement.

n_minus_2_modest.interpretation: Against an N-2 base (2024-02, 20.22) the movement is -10.1% — much smaller than the -42.5% change vs N-1. The N-1 comparison is distorted by an anomalous base.

anomaly_report.suggested_caveat: The comparison base (2025-02) is 4.8 standard deviations above the mean of the prior 12 months (31.64 vs rolling mean 22.26), so the year-on-year movement mechanically overstates the underlying trend. Production indicators (IPI -3.9% YoY) show no corresponding shock to the -42.1% trade value change, a divergence consistent with a composition or base effect. Value fell by 42.1% while volume moved -17.4%, indicating a unit-value or mix shift. 95.2% of the absolute variation is attributable to 2 partners (United States, Switzerland). Against the N-2 base (2024-02) the change is -10.1%, versus -42.5% vs N-1 — a more representative read of the underlying trend.
